findings.problem: Sanitization at the causal trigger: the affair letter is introduced with one concrete line and then collapsed into summary ('Then the rest') at the exact beat where sexual specificity drives legal and reputational stakes. The scene flinches from detail when consequence is being set.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the reveal at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:49 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51 so Peter reads at least two additional concrete lines from Elsie's note (physical detail plus relational intent), then adjust his reaction at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:55 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:65 to reference those specifics rather than a generic accusation.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised reveal block anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:47 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:55 contains at least three quoted letter details (including one explicitly physical and one explicitly relational) and Peter's threat language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:63 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:66 names at least one of those details; FAIL if the scene still jumps from one quoted line to summary phrasing.

findings.problem: The chapter's two Peter confrontations repeat nearly the same custody/parish threat package instead of escalating in kind. Scene three reiterates scene one's leverage more than it changes the situation, which dulls momentum.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the threat architecture between the first confrontation anchors at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:63 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:86 and the street confrontation anchors at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:275 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:340: either compress first-scene legal detail and save specifics for the street, or keep first-scene threat broad and make second-scene threat materially new (for example, he has already contacted a party, produced a filed draft, or invokes a new leverage channel).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can point to one unique, concrete escalation between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:275 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:340 that did not appear between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:63 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:86 (beyond restating custody/morality/parish), and if duplicate threat language is reduced to one core idea per scene; FAIL if both scenes still list the same threat bundle in near-parallel wording.

findings.problem: Peter's register stays in a single lectern-formal mode for too long under street-pressure conflict. Low-contraction formality is canonical for him, but the scene underuses his documented stress shift into rupture/contempt, so the confrontation voice flattens into repeated legal diction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep Peter's formal baseline between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:269 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:340, but add at least one stress fracture where syntax breaks (interruption, fragment, or clipped contempt) and trim repeated formal scaffolding (e.g., stacked 'I shall'/'I file' constructions) so his control visibly destabilizes before he reasserts it.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Peter has at least one line between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:299 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:322 that departs from full administrative cadence (broken syntax or targeted contempt) while still sounding like Peter, and if no more than one ceremonially formal lead-in ('I shall'/'I file') remains between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:340; FAIL if his street confrontation remains uniformly formal throughout.

findings.problem: A recurring negation-correction template ('Not X, not Y') appears three times, turning a once-effective emphasis move into a chapter tic. The repetition signals autopilot instead of intentional variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least two of the repeated constructions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:323 into different sentence engines (for example: concrete action beat, bodily sensation, or direct interior statement) rather than mirrored negation pairs.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter contains no more than one 'Not X, not Y' construction across the cited lines and each replacement expresses contrast through a distinct syntax pattern; FAIL if two or more of those beats retain the same negation template.

findings.problem: The van-arrival setup runs for more than ten consecutive short paragraphs with external staging and dialogue before returning to interior fear at line 283. That stretch reads screenplayed rather than close-third embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Inject Noreen interiority beats into the run between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:281 (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment), ideally before Peter's doorway block at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:265 and again before the explicit fear line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the span between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:281 contains at least two explicit interiority beats from Noreen, with no gap longer than five paragraphs between interior markers; FAIL if interior access still resumes only at or after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283.

findings.problem: Mechanical over-literal reuse of stress-tell/checklist cadence: multiple consecutive sections rely on instruction lists and procedural inventories in nearly identical rhythm, which flattens scene texture and makes character voices feel more tactical than lived.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reshape the noon planning block at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:148, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:206, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:228 by breaking at least one checklist into conflict or sensory interruption, while preserving critical logistics; keep one list for clarity and vary the others into interaction-driven beats.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised planning section contains no more than one uninterrupted checklist-style instruction run longer than three clauses, and at least two interruptions (emotion, sensory detail, or interpersonal pushback) appear between logistical directives between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:206 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:228; FAIL if the sequence remains mostly list cadence end-to-end.

summary: Chapter 16 lands required state change and continuity beats, but it does not clear review: the affair-reveal beat sanitizes key causal intimacy detail, Peter's threat scenes are structurally repetitive, dialogue pressure flattens into prolonged legal diction, prose falls into repeated negation formulas, the van-arrival run drops interiority for 10+ paragraphs, and the planning middle leans on repetitive checklist cadence.
